# Release 1.0.6 (November 27. 2023)

Code Changes:

- Use PEP-484 compatible exports to satisfy static type checkers.

# Release 1.0.5 (November 22. 2023)

Code Changes:

- Add `py.typed` file for better typing support. Thanks! @Eisfunke
- Use explicit imports: `from .lib import` is now `from sty.lib import`.
- More and better doc-strings. `help(x)` should be much more useful now.

Other:

- Add svg logo. Thanks! @kubinka0505
- Replace `pipenv` with `poetry`.
- Remove `make.py` build system.
- Remove all dev dependencies.

# Description

Sty's goal is to provide Python with a simple, customizable and performant string styling markup, which
is decoupled from color palettes and terminal implementations.

- Sty supports 3/4bit, 8bit and 24bit (truecolor/RGB) colors as well as effects like `bold`, `italic`, `underline`, etc.
- Sty should work on most Unix platforms with most terminals. It works with recent Windows terminals. Window legacy terminal (cmd) needs a [shim](https://github.com/feluxe/sty/issues/2#issuecomment-501890699) to work.
- Sty comes with default color palettes and renderers, but you can easily replace/customize them, without touching the markup in your code.
- Sty allows you to mute/unmute all styles in your codebase.
- Sty provides high access performance for all styling rules.
- Sty is fully typed, you should get good editor support for it.
- Sty does not implicitly mess with globals. E.g.: `colorama` overrides `sys.stdout` which causes a lot of trouble.
- Sty has no dependencies.
- Sty follows [semver](https://semver.org/).
- Sty will support Python `>=3.7` for as long as possible.

If you run into compatibility problems with sty, please file an [issue](https://github.com/feluxe/sty/issues)!

# Code Example

```python
from sty import fg, bg, ef, rs

foo = fg.red + 'This is red text!' + fg.rs
bar = bg.blue + 'This has a blue background!' + bg.rs
baz = ef.italic + 'This is italic text' + rs.italic
qux = fg(201) + 'This is pink text using 8bit colors' + fg.rs
qui = fg(255, 10, 10) + 'This is red text using 24bit colors.' + fg.rs

# Add custom colors:

from sty import Style, RgbFg

fg.orange = Style(RgbFg(255, 150, 50))

buf = fg.orange + 'Yay, Im orange.' + fg.rs

print(foo, bar, baz, qux, qui, buf, sep='\n')
```

The code above will print like this in the terminal:

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/feluxe/sty/master/assets/README_example.png
:alt: example
:target: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/feluxe/sty/master/assets/README_example.png
:width: 600px
```

You can use the `Register` class or the default registers `FgRegister`, `BgRegister`, `EfRegister` and `RsRegister` to create your own registers:

```python
# Extending the default FgRegister

from sty import FgRegister, Style, RgbFg, Sgr

class MyFgRegister(FgRegister):

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()

        self.purple = Style(Sgr(35))
        self.blue = Style(Sgr(34))
        self.orange = Style(RgbFg(255, 128, 0))
        # ...

 fg = MyFgRegister()
```

# Documentation

Documentation-Website: <https://sty.mewo.dev>

Documentation-Website-Source: <https://github.com/feluxe/sty-docs>

modules.name: sty.renderfunc
documentation:
A selection of render functions.

These functions generate the escape-sequences that trigger certain colors/effects in
the terminals.

functions.name: sty.renderfunc.sgr
documentation: Create a SGR escape sequence.

functions.name: sty.renderfunc.eightbit_fg
documentation: Create a 8bit (256-color) foreground escape sequence.

functions.name: sty.renderfunc.eightbit_bg
documentation: Create a 8bit (256-color) background escape sequence.

functions.name: sty.renderfunc.rgb_fg
documentation: Create a 24bit (true color) foreground escape sequence.

functions.name: sty.renderfunc.rgb_bg
documentation: Create a 24bit (true color) background escape sequence.

modules.name: sty.lib
documentation:
Utility funcitons go here.
This module is private. You shouldn't import it when using sty.

functions.name: sty.lib.mute
documentation:
Use this function to mute multiple register-objects at once.

```
param objects

Pass multiple register-objects to the function.
```

functions.name: sty.lib.unmute
documentation:
Use this function to unmute multiple register-objects at once.

```
param objects

Pass multiple register-objects to the function.
```

modules.name: sty
documentation:
String styling for your terminal.

Documentation: https://sty.mewo.dev
Source Code: https://github.com/feluxe/sty

Example: Print bold text with red foreground, green background and reset:

    from sty import fg, bg, ef, rs

    print(f"{ef.b}{fg.red}{bg.green}My String{rs.all}")

Example: Create custom style:

    from sty import RgbFg, Style

    fg.orange = Style(RgbFg(255, 150, 50))

Example: Create your own register:

    from sty import FgRegister, RgbFg, Sgr

    class MyFgRegister(FgRegister):
        def __init__(self):
            super().__init__()

            self.purple = Style(Sgr(35))
            self.blue = Style(Sgr(34))
            # ...

    fg = MyFgRegister()

For more info see the docs.

modules.name: sty.rendertype
documentation:
This module contains the default render types.

These can be used to create register classes and custom styling rules.

classes.name: sty.rendertype.Sgr
documentation:
Define SGR styling rule.

More info about SGR parameters: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#SGR>

```
param num

A SGR number.
```

classes.name: sty.rendertype.EightbitFg
documentation:
Define Eightbit Foreground.

More info about 8-bit terminal colors: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#8-bit>

```
param num

Eightbit number.
```

classes.name: sty.rendertype.EightbitBg
documentation:
Define Eightbit Background.

More info about 8-bit terminal colors: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#8-bit>

```
param num

Eightbit number.
```

classes.name: sty.rendertype.RgbFg
documentation:
Define RGB Foreground.

More info about 24-bit terminal colors: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#24-bit>

```
param r

Red.

param g

Green.

param b

Blue.
```

classes.name: sty.rendertype.RgbBg
documentation:
Define RGB Background.

More info about 24-bit terminal colors: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#24-bit>

```
param r

Red.

param g

Green.

param b

Blue.
```

modules.name: sty.primitive
documentation: The Register class: Sty's heart.

classes.name: sty.primitive.Style
documentation:
This type stores the different styling rules for the registers and the resulting
ANSI-sequence as a string.

For example:

    fg.orange = Style(RgbFg(1,5,10), Sgr(1))

    isinstance(fg.orange, Style) # True

    isinstance(fg.orange, str) # True

    str(fg.orange) # '[38;2;1;5;10m[1m' (The ASNI sequence for orange and bold)

classes.name: sty.primitive.Register

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.__call__
documentation: This function is to handle calls such as `fg(42)`, `bg(102, 49, 42)`, `fg('red')`.

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.set_eightbit_call
documentation:
You can call a register-object directly. A call like this `fg(144)`
is a Eightbit-call. With this method you can define the render-type for such calls.

```
param rendertype

The new rendertype that is used for Eightbit-calls.
```

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.set_rgb_call
documentation:
You can call a register-object directly. A call like this `fg(10, 42, 255)`
is a RGB-call. With this method you can define the render-type for such calls.

```
param rendertype

The new rendertype that is used for RGB-calls.
```

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.set_renderfunc
documentation:
With this method you can add or replace render-functions for a given register-object:

```
param rendertype

The render type for which the new renderfunc is used.

param func

The new render function.
```

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.mute
documentation:
Sometimes it is useful to disable the formatting for a register-object. You can
do so by invoking this method.

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.unmute
documentation: Use this method to unmute a previously muted register object.

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.as_dict
documentation: Export color register as dict.

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.as_namedtuple
documentation: Export color register as namedtuple.

methods.name: sty.primitive.Register.copy
documentation: Make a deepcopy of a register-object.

This is the base Register class. All default registers (fg, bg, ef, rs) are
created from this class. You can use it to create your own custom registers.

modules.name: sty.register
documentation: These are the default registers that sty provides out of the box.

classes.name: sty.register.EfRegister
documentation:
The default effect register.

Instances from this class can be used to create effects like 'bold', 'dim',
'italic', 'blink', etc.

For example:

    print(f"{ef.bold}Bold Text{ef.rs}")
    print(f"{ef.italic}Italic Text{ef.rs}")

classes.name: sty.register.FgRegister
documentation:
The default 'foreground register'.

Instances from this class can be used to create colored text.

For example:

    print(f"{fg.red}Red Text{fg.rs}")
    print(f"{fg.green}Green Text{fg.rs}")

classes.name: sty.register.BgRegister
documentation:
The default 'background register'.

Instances from this class can be used to create text with colored backgrounds.

For example:

    print(f"{bg.red}Text With Red Background{bg.rs}")
    print(f"{bg.green}Text With Green Background{bg.rs}")

classes.name: sty.register.RsRegister
documentation:
The default 'reset register'.

Instances from this class can be used to reset single effects, foregrounds
and backgrounds.

For example:

    print(f"{ef.italic}Italic Text{rs.italic}")
    print(f"{ef.underl}Underlined Text{rs.underl}")
    print(f"{fg.red}{ef.italic}Red Italic Text{rs.all}")
